CHAPTER IX. Moses rehearseth their rebellions, EAR, 0 Israel; Thou art to pass over Jordan this day, to go in to possess nations greater and mightier than thyself, cities great and fenced up to heaven, ~

“Beware Lest Thou Forget the Lord” By Elder Dean L. Larsen Of the Presidency of the Seventy We welcome the Brethren whom you have sustained this morning as new members of the Seventies Quorums. They are men of faith and devotion with a broad background of experience in Church leadership. Their call is a reflection of the continuing growth of the Church worldwide. As the tribes of ancient Israel…

23 Nor that Moses said,‘ Not for thy righteous- Nor that Moses said to Israel, ‘ Not for thy 23 ness or for the uprightness of thine heart dost | righteousness nor for the uprightness of thine thou go in to inherit these nations, but because | heart dost thou go in to inherit these nations, but He loved thy fathers and because He would | because He loved thy fathers and because He keep the oath.’…

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Ibn Ezra on Deuteronomy 10:6:1
matches.x: AND THE CHILDREN OF ISRAEL JOURNEYED FROM BEEROTH-BENEJAAKAN TO MOSERAH; THERE AARON DIED. This verse is placed here21Amidst the account of the golden calf. because Moses said, And I prayed for Aaron also the same time (Deut. 9:20).22Saying, And I prayed for Aaron also at that time, Moses indicated that Aaron did not go along with those who worshipped the calf. Moses indicated that Aaron did not…
